NAME

client.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_info
Per-client state structure for server-side client management. struct app_client_audio_packet_t
Audio packet for async transmission. struct app_client
Client-side application state.

Macros


#define APP_CLIENT_AUDIO_QUEUE_SIZE 256

Typedefs


typedef struct client_info client_info_t
Per-client state structure for server-side client management. typedef struct app_client app_client_t
Client-side application state.

Enumerations


enum participant_type_t { PARTICIPANT_TYPE_NETWORK, PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY }
Participant type for distinguishing network vs memory participants.

Functions


app_client_t * app_client_create (void)
Create and initialize client application context. void app_client_destroy (app_client_t **client_ptr)
Destroy client application context and free all resources.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_CLIENT_AUDIO_QUEUE_SIZE 256

Definition at line 218 of file include/ascii-chat/network/client.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct app_client app_client_t

Client-side application state. Transport-agnostic container for application-layer state previously mixed in tcp_client_t. Holds audio queues, thread handles, crypto context, and display state. Maintains references to active network client and transport.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum participant_type_t

Participant type for distinguishing network vs memory participants. Network participants communicate over TCP/IP or WebRTC, while memory participants inject media directly into the host's mixer (used when the host participates in the session with their own webcam/audio).

Enumerator

PARTICIPANT_TYPE_NETWORK
PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY

Definition at line 34 of file include/ascii-chat/network/client.h.

34              {
35   PARTICIPANT_TYPE_NETWORK, // Remote participant via socket/transport
36   PARTICIPANT_TYPE_MEMORY   // Local host participant (direct memory access)
37 } participant_type_t;










Function
  Documentation




app_client_t * app_client_create (void )


Create and initialize client application context.


Returns


Pointer to initialized context, or NULL on failure


Definition at line 24 of file
  lib/network/client.c.


24                                       {
25   app_client_t *client = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(app_client_t), app_client_t *);
26   if (!client) {
27     log_error("Failed to allocate app_client_t");
28     return NULL;
29   }
30 
31   // Zero-initialize all fields
32   memset(client, 0, sizeof(*client));
33 
34   /* Transport */
35   client->active_transport = NULL;
36   client->transport_type = ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP;
37   client->tcp_client = NULL;
38   client->ws_client = NULL;
39 
40   /* Audio State */
41   memset(&client->audio_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->audio_ctx));
42   memset(client->audio_send_queue, 0, sizeof(client->audio_send_queue));
43   client->audio_send_queue_head = 0;
44   client->audio_send_queue_tail = 0;
45   client->audio_send_queue_initialized = false;
46   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_sender_should_exit, false);
47   client->audio_capture_thread_created = false;
48   client->audio_sender_thread_created = false;
49   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_capture_thread_exited, false);
50 
51   // Initialize audio queue mutex and condition variable
52   if (mutex_init(&client->audio_send_queue_mutex, "audio_queue") != 0) {
53     log_error("Failed to initialize audio queue mutex");
54     SAFE_FREE(client);
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   if (cond_init(&client->audio_send_queue_cond, "audio_queue") != 0) {
59     log_error("Failed to initialize audio queue cond");
60     mutex_destroy(&client->audio_send_queue_mutex);
61     SAFE_FREE(client);
62     return NULL;
63   }
64 
65   /* Protocol State */
66   client->data_thread_created = false;
67   atomic_store_bool(&client->data_thread_exited, false);
68   client->last_active_count = 0;
69   client->server_state_initialized = false;
70   client->should_clear_before_next_frame = false;
71   client->my_client_id = 0;
72   client->encryption_enabled = false;
73 
74   /* Capture State */
75   client->capture_thread_created = false;
76   atomic_store_bool(&client->capture_thread_exited, false);
77 
78   /* Keepalive State */
79   client->ping_thread_created = false;
80   atomic_store_bool(&client->ping_thread_exited, false);
81 
82   /* Display State */
83   client->has_tty = false;
84   atomic_store_bool(&client->is_first_frame_of_connection, false);
85   memset(&client->tty_info, 0, sizeof(client->tty_info));
86 
87   /* Crypto State */
88   memset(&client->crypto_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_ctx));
89   client->crypto_initialized = false;
90 
91   // Register atomic fields for sync state monitoring with fully descriptive names
92   char atomic_name[256];
93 
94   // Audio subsystem control and status
95   snprintf(atomic_name, sizeof(atomic_name), "app_client.audio_sender_thread_should_exit_signal");
96   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&client->audio_sender_should_exit, atomic_name, NULL);
97 
98   snprintf(atomic_name, sizeof(atomic_name), "app_client.audio_capture_thread_has_exited_status");
99   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&client->audio_capture_thread_exited, atomic_name, NULL);
100 
101   // Protocol and network data handling
102   snprintf(atomic_name, sizeof(atomic_name), "app_client.protocol_data_dispatch_thread_has_exited_status");
103   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&client->data_thread_exited, atomic_name, NULL);
104 
105   // Media capture control and status
106   snprintf(atomic_name, sizeof(atomic_name), "app_client.media_capture_thread_has_exited_status");
107   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&client->capture_thread_exited, atomic_name, NULL);
108 
109   // Keepalive and connection health monitoring
110   snprintf(atomic_name, sizeof(atomic_name), "app_client.keepalive_ping_thread_has_exited_status");
111   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&client->ping_thread_exited, atomic_name, NULL);
112 
113   // Display frame sequencing
114   snprintf(atomic_name, sizeof(atomic_name), "app_client.is_first_frame_of_new_connection_flag");
115   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&client->is_first_frame_of_connection, atomic_name, NULL);
116 
117   log_debug("App client created");
118 
119   return client;
120 }






References ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP, atomic_store_bool(),
    cond_init(), log_debug, log_error,
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC,
    SAFE_FREE, and SAFE_MALLOC.






void
  app_client_destroy (app_client_t ** client_ptr)


Destroy client application context and free all resources.


Parameters


client_ptr Pointer to client pointer (set to NULL
  after free)


Definition at line 125 of file
  lib/network/client.c.


125                                                    {
126   if (!client_ptr || !*client_ptr) {
127     return; // No-op if NULL
128   }
129 
130   app_client_t *client = *client_ptr;
131 
132   log_debug("Destroying app client");
133 
134   // Unregister atomic fields
135   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&client->audio_sender_should_exit);
136   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&client->audio_capture_thread_exited);
137   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&client->data_thread_exited);
138   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&client->capture_thread_exited);
139   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&client->ping_thread_exited);
140   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&client->is_first_frame_of_connection);
141 
142   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
143   mutex_destroy(&client->audio_send_queue_mutex);
144   cond_destroy(&client->audio_send_queue_cond);
145 
146   // Note: Network clients (tcp_client, ws_client) are not destroyed here.
147   // They should be destroyed separately in connection_context_cleanup()
148   // to maintain proper lifecycle management.
149 
150   SAFE_FREE(*client_ptr);
151 }






References cond_destroy(), log_debug,
    mutex_destroy(), NAMED_UNREGISTER, and SAFE_FREE.
